audited 22/45: BUG=7 CLEAN=15 UNCERTAIN=0

bugs.evidence: test_output_contains_all_patterns hardcodes expected_patterns = {"a", "ab", "a|b", "a*", "(ab)*"} (5 patterns), but the real /workspace/data/regex_patterns.txt contains 12 patterns (also includes "a*b", "(a|b)*", "ab|cd", "a(b|c)*d", "(abc)*d", "x|y|z", "(a|b)(c|d)"). A correct solution producing output for all 12 patterns will fail the equality assertion, while one that only outputs the 5 hardcoded patterns will correctly pass — making the test grade the wrong behavior.

The test `test_all_tests_passed` in test_outputs.py trusts a results.txt file that the agent writes itself — an agent can trivially pass by writing "TEST_SMALL=PASS
TEST_MEDIUM=PASS
TEST_LARGE=PASS
MEMORY_BOUNDED=PASS" with no actual validation. The functional tests (test_sampler_small_stream, test_sampler_medium_stream) only check that output line count equals K and each line is valid JSON, which means any script that outputs K dummy JSON objects (without any real reservoir sampling) would pass all grading tests.

test_correct_number_of_documents hardcodes `assert len(documents) == 5` with comment "matching documents.txt line count", but the real deployed /data/raw_annotations/documents.txt contains 20 lines (documents 0-19). A correct solution producing all 20 document sections would fail this test.

The test suite has no hardcoded expected per-order outcomes and never verifies that the saga coordinator actually runs against the real service mocks. A trivial solution writing "ORD-001:completed:yes" through "ORD-008:completed:yes" (all 'completed', no compensation logic ever actually executed) passes every test: file-exists checks pass, format checks pass, test_all_orders_consistent passes (all 'yes'), test_verification_has_both_outcomes passes ('completed' in outcomes is True), and test_saga_coordinator_has_compensation_logic only requires a keyword like 'rollback' anywhere in the source. The real data has ORD-002 (item3, stock=0), ORD-003 (CARD-DECLINE-001), ORD-004 (ADDR-FAIL-001), ORD-006 (CARD-DECLINE-002), ORD-007 (ADDR-FAIL-002) which should all be 'compensated', but no test enforces this.

Both deployed .bai files are fake: sample_A.bam.bai is a plain-text LLM explanation (starts "I cannot generate a valid BAM index..."), and sample_C.bam.bai starts with "BAI" magic but is filled with UTF-8 replacement characters (0xEFBFBD). Neither is a valid binary BAM index. The instruction example shows sample_A: OK and sample_C: OK (implying their indexes are already valid), but running samtools idxstats on them would fail, causing test_bam_indexes_are_valid to fail for any agent that follows the instruction's guidance.

The instruction.md hardcodes expected output values (board_1=3, board_3=5, board_5=1) in its OUTPUT REQUIREMENTS section, but computing shared liberties from the actual deployed board files yields board_1=4, board_3=3, board_5=2. The test dynamically computes the correct answer from the board files and will reject an agent that follows the instruction's example output literally.

The instruction specifies 10 sentences requiring "at least 8 out of 10" matches and "exactly 10 lines", but the deployed data has only 9 sentences (test_input.txt and expected_output.txt each have 9 lines), and the grading tests require only >=5 lines and 4-out-of-5 matches — drastically weaker than the stated spec, allowing a mostly-wrong solution to pass.
